rule_text: A trader should avoid trading or reduce position sizing during market equilibrium conditions characterized by sideways price action, flat intermediate-term moving averages, and support/resistance ranges, as these conditions indicate accumulation by larger players rather than genuine directional moves.
entry_conditions: Intermediate MA (20-period SMA) slope < 0.5% over last 10 bars
Price oscillating within defined support/resistance band (range < 2% of price)
Price crossing intermediate MA on both sides within last 5 bars
ATR < 20th percentile (low volatility confirmation)
exit_conditions: Do not enter; skip trade entirely
hallucination_issues: Specific numeric thresholds (0.5%, 2%, 20th percentile) are not mentioned in source text
20-period SMA specification not provided in original excerpt
FUTURES instrument type not mentioned or implied in source text
ATR condition added without source support
haiku_reason: All four entry conditions are specific, quantifiable metrics (MA slope percentage, price range percentage, bar counts, ATR percentile) that can be programmatically detected from OHLC data, and the exit condition (skip trade) is an actionable directive when conditions are met.

rule_text: When trading against clean technical levels, account for actual risk as a multiple of intended risk due to slippage and stop-triggering volatility, and size positions accordingly to avoid catastrophic losses.
entry_conditions: Price approaches or tests a clean technical level (support/resistance)
Stop order is placed just beyond the level
Position size is calculated assuming normal slippage
exit_conditions: Exit at breakout beyond level if level holds
Exit on reversal signal if level holds
hallucination_issues: Exit conditions not mentioned in source text - text warns against trading against levels, doesn't provide exit strategies
Instrument specification as 'FUTURES' not supported by source text
Specific parameter values (10x multiplier, 0.1 reduction factor) are reasonable inferences but not explicitly stated
Stop placement parameter not directly specified in source
haiku_reason: Entry conditions specify testable market events (price tests technical levels, stop placement beyond level, position sizing based on calculated multiples) that can be detected programmatically from price data and trader rules, though the definition of 'clean technical level' would require clarification.

source_citation: The Art and Science of Technical Analysis, Adam Grimes, Ch. P1:OTA, pp. 163-204
raw_chunk_text:
One pattern to avoid is buying against multiple (three or more) tests of support in
a pullback, as this pattern is often more indicative of an impending failure. The opera-
tiveconcepthereisusuallylow
rule_text: Avoid buying pullbacks that test support three or more times, as multiple tests with lower highs indicate weakening buyer interest and higher probability of failure.
entry_conditions: Price is in an uptrend
Price has pulled back to support
Support level has been tested 2 or more times in the last 5-10 bars
High of the current bounce is lower than the previous bounce high
exit_conditions: Do not enter; wait for alternative setup
hallucination_issues: Text says 'three or more' tests but parameters use 'min_tests_to_reject: 2'
Specific values like 'lookback_bars_for_tests: 5' are not mentioned in source
Instrument specified as 'FUTURES' but source text is generic to all markets
Entry conditions add specific technical details not explicitly stated in source
haiku_reason: All four entry conditions are specific and programmable: uptrend detection, pullback-to-support identification, support test counting within a bar window, and lower-high comparison are all quantifiable from OHLC data.

Stop The general rule of stops in countertrend trades applies here: these stops must
be respected without exception. There are two reasonable stops for this pattern. One
choiceistosimplyputthestopbeyo
rule_text: A trader should place stops at defined price levels that reflect either a decisive trend reversal failure (conservative) or an intermediate pullback threshold (aggressive), and must execute these stops without exception in countertrend trades.
entry_conditions: Countertrend trade setup identified
Prior trend extreme (swing high in downtrend or swing low in uptrend) established
exit_conditions: Stop triggered if price closes beyond the prior trend extreme
Trend reversal has decisively failed when extreme is violated
hallucination_issues: Instrument specified as 'FUTURES' but source text makes no mention of specific instruments
Entry conditions are reasonably inferred but not explicitly stated in the excerpt
Parameter values like 'buffer_pips_above_extreme: 0' are not specified in source
Position size adjustment details are inferred from general mention of 'larger position sizes'
haiku_reason: The signal contains specific, programmable entry conditions (countertrend setup identification and prior trend extreme detection) and a clear exit rule (price closes beyond prior extreme), which can be implemented as code logic against historical price data.

rule_text: A trader should reject or skip trades that align with a smaller timeframe pattern when a higher timeframe chart shows a conflicting bias or incomplete consolidation pattern.
entry_conditions: Lower timeframe shows consolidation breakout signal (e.g., daily chart break above resistance)
Higher timeframe (weekly or monthly) shows unresolved or ambiguous consolidation pattern
Higher timeframe bias is opposite to lower timeframe directional signal
exit_conditions: Do not enter; wait for higher timeframe consolidation to resolve with matching bias
hallucination_issues: Parameters contain specific numeric values (52 bars, 3.0%, 8 bars) not mentioned in source text
Instrument specified as 'FUTURES' but source text doesn't specify any particular instrument type
Exit conditions infer waiting for resolution but source doesn't explicitly state this strategy
haiku_reason: The signal specifies concrete entry conditions (consolidation breakout on lower timeframe, unresolved consolidation on higher timeframe, opposite bias detection) that can be operationalized through price level analysis and pattern recognition on multi-timeframe data.

rule_text: Avoid taking partial exits at a loss, as locking in losses anchors P&L in the loss space and makes recovery difficult; instead, exit the entire position at a small loss rather than deleveraging through partial loss exits.
entry_conditions: Position is established with defined entry price
Trade moves into loss territory (current price < entry price)
exit_conditions: Exit entire position when unrealized loss reaches 0.5%
Do not take any partial profits or partial exits while in loss state
hallucination_issues: Specific 0.5% loss threshold not mentioned in original text
Instrument type 'FUTURES' not specified in source
Exact exit conditions with numerical thresholds are added without source support
haiku_reason: The signal has specific, measurable entry conditions (position established, unrealized loss exists) and quantifiable exit triggers (0.5% loss threshold) that a programmer could implement with market data and portfolio state monitoring.

rule_text: Build a profit cushion by scaling out of winning positions at predefined profit targets and re-entering at lower prices within range-bound markets, reducing ultimate loss exposure if the trade fails.
entry_conditions: Position established in identified accumulation/range
Price reaches 1.0X initial risk in profit
exit_conditions: Exit 50% at 1.0X profit
Stop loss on remaining 50% at original stop level
One re-entry allowed if price returns to entry zone
hallucination_issues: ATR parameter (0.5 ATR for re-entry zone) not mentioned in source text
FUTURES instrument specification not supported by source text - appears generic
max_re_entry_attempts set to 1 but source suggests 'perhaps several times'
haiku_reason: The signal contains specific, actionable conditions that can be coded: detecting accumulation/range zones, triggering exits at 1.0X profit threshold, managing stop losses at original levels, and re-entry logic when price returns to entry zone.

Trade Reaches a Risk Multiple Target For many technical traders, once the
trade has made a significant move into the profit zone, it rarely makes sense to take a
full-sized loss on the trade. Traders
rule_text: Once a trade has moved a distance equal to the initial risk amount in the profitable direction, tighten the stop loss to reduce risk exposure, as the pattern is validating as expected.
entry_conditions: Trade entered with defined stop loss at initial_risk distance
Market price moves exactly 1.0x initial_risk in profitable direction
exit_conditions: If stop is hit at entry level (or entry + buffer), exit trade
Preserve remaining profit equal to 1.0x initial risk
hallucination_issues: Instrument specified as 'FUTURES' but excerpt mentions '$50.00 market' without specifying instrument type
Target regimes 'TRENDING' and 'ANY' are not mentioned in the source text
New stop placement specified as 'entry_price + 1_pip_buffer' but source says 'just under your entry point or even higher' - more vague than specified
Exit conditions are inferred logically but not explicitly stated in the source
haiku_reason: The signal has specific, actionable entry conditions (stop loss at initial_risk distance and price movement of 1.0x initial_risk) that can be programmatically detected from market data and portfolio state.

rule_text: In parabolic, runaway uptrends, use tight stops placed under the previous day's low to protect gains while staying in the accelerating trend.

Parabolic acceleration detected (rate of price increase accelerates)
Sharp upward momentum confirmed
exit_conditions: Close below previous day's low
Stop hit

rule_text: Never widen stops in the direction of increasing risk; maintain the initial stop loss calculated at trade entry based on fixed risk parameters, and only tighten stops after momentum confirms trend development in the trade's favor.
entry_conditions: Trade initiated with fixed stop loss
Position size calculated based on initial stop distance
Stop loss level documented at entry
exit_conditions: Stop loss hit at original level
Profit target hit

haiku_reason: The entry conditions specify concrete, detectable states (fixed stop loss documented, position size based on initial stop distance) that a programmer could verify from trade records and market data, and the exit conditions are objectively measurable price levels.

source_citation: The Art and Science of Technical Analysis, Adam Grimes, Ch. P1:OTA, pp. 247–278
rule_text: Limit aggregate risk exposure across correlated asset groups to prevent portfolio-wide losses when positions move in unison during adverse events.
entry_conditions: New position entry is contemplated in an asset group (precious metals, petroleum, grains, currencies with regional ties, etc.)
Existing positions in same or correlated group are already held
Aggregate notional risk across correlated group calculated
exit_conditions: Exit or reduce position when aggregate correlated group risk exceeds 1.5X single position risk limit
hallucination_issues: Parameter 'correlation_threshold_for_grouping': 0.7 not mentioned in source text
Parameter 'worst_case_loss_scenario_percentile': 0.99 not mentioned in source text
Max risk multiple should be range 1.5X-2X, not fixed at 1.5X
Target regimes HIGH_VOL and ANY not specified in source text
Entry/exit conditions are reasonable inferences but not explicitly stated
haiku_reason: The signal has specific, actionable entry conditions (new position in correlated asset group + existing positions in same group + aggregate risk calculation) and a quantifiable exit trigger (aggregate risk exceeding 1.5X limit) that can be detected from portfolio state and notional exposure data.

rule_text: Position size should be scaled as a fixed percentage of current account equity rather than a fixed dollar amount, so that risk exposure remains proportional to account growth or decline.
entry_conditions: Valid trade signal generated by system
Account equity is measurable and current
exit_conditions: Stop loss triggered at designated level
Profit target reached
hallucination_issues: Parameters specify 1% risk when excerpt mentions 2% risk
Exit conditions not mentioned in source text
Instrument specified as FUTURES but source doesn't mention specific instrument type
Entry conditions are reasonable inferences but not explicitly stated
haiku_reason: The rule has specific, actionable entry conditions (valid trade signal + measurable current equity) that a programmer could detect and implement as code to automatically scale position sizes as a percentage of account equity.

rule_text: A trader should avoid or reduce confidence in long setups when successive bounces off support terminate at progressively lower price levels, as diminishing upswing heights indicate weakening buying pressure.
entry_conditions: Price bounces off support level
Compare height of current bounce to previous bounce
Current bounce high < previous bounce high
exit_conditions: Do not enter; skip setup entirely if deterioration pattern exists
hallucination_issues: Specific numerical parameters (consecutive_lower_highs_required: 2, minimum_bounce_lookback_periods: 2, rejection_threshold_pct: 0.5) are not mentioned in the source text
Instrument is specified as 'FUTURES' but the example discusses JPM (a stock), and the text mentions both stock and futures traders use similar terminology
Signal category 'TREND|REVERSION' is not explicitly stated in the source
haiku_reason: The signal contains three specific, actionable entry conditions that a programmer can operationalize: identifying support level bounces, measuring bounce heights numerically, and comparing successive bounce highs using quantifiable price data.
